key_points: Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic states that every composite number has a unique prime factorisation
HCF and LCM are found using prime factorisation
Rational numbers have terminating or repeating decimal expansions
Decimal nature depends on prime factors of denominator
formulas: HCF × LCM = product of two numbers
Terminating decimal → denominator has only 2 and/or 5 as prime factors
exam_tips: Always reduce fractions before checking decimal expansion
Use lowest powers for HCF and highest powers for LCM
